name: CMN Hypha
synopsis: CMN reference client CLI — releases spores, queries synapses, resolves bonds, and manages the local mycelium
intent: Provide the reference CMN client: release signed spore manifests, query Synapse indexers for discovery, resolve bond URIs, verify content hashes, and manage the local development workflow
Enable any developer to publish, discover, and consume spores from the command line — the primary interface for interacting with the CMN network
mutations: Split monolithic source files into focused modules — visitor, spore, mycelium, cli, and auth each become separate module directories with dedicated files per command
Add Synapse data fallback — when a domain is unreachable, fetch spore and mycelium data from a trusted Synapse indexer with key-based verification, configurable via synapse witness fallback policy
Security hardening — reject symlinks at release and extraction, detect malicious archives in absorb and auto-mark as toxic, replace string-based error prefixes with typed ExtractError enum
Separate spawned_from tracking from spore.core.json — grow and absorb now read the upstream spore from .cmn/spawned-from/spore.json, release writes there instead of modifying spore.core.json
grow --bond integration — after syncing upstream, automatically check bond dependencies and fetch updated bonds via Synapse lineage
Add delta archive transfers — endpoint-driven archive deltas with zstd compression for efficient updates when the base hash is available
Mycelium management — add bio and nutrients fields to mycelium CLI, pulse now supports all document types (spore, taste, mycelium) not just mycelium
Replace docgen binary with --help-markdown flag built into the main binary via agent-first-data's clap-markdown integration

bonds.reason: Implements the CMN protocol specification — reference client CLI
All CLI output and protocol fields use Agent-First Data suffix conventions
